rationale: The cited paper is used for more than background listing: it is invoked to interpret a core empirical result about why loss-framed (punitive) incentives did not outperform standard bonus incentives. In the discussion, the authors explicitly connect their finding to prior evidence that loss aversion may not apply to intended exchangeable money, citing Novemsky and Kahneman (2005). This gives the cited work a clear functional role in the paper's theoretical interpretation of results in a new salesforce setting.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Novemsky and Kahneman's boundary condition on loss aversion to a field sales compensation context. It uses that account to explain the observed weak incremental effect of punitive, money-based incentive framing.
